---
title: SMTP
icon: At
description: Send emails via SMTP with Nodemailer
---

import {
  CodeBlockTab,
  CodeBlockTabs,
  CodeBlockTabsList,
  CodeBlockTabsTrigger,
} from 'fumadocs-ui/components/codeblock';
import { TypeTable } from 'fumadocs-ui/components/type-table';

The SMTP transport is an official Better-Notify package backed by Nodemailer. Use it when your provider gives you SMTP credentials and you want a straightforward way to send Better-Notify email routes.

It is a thin delivery adapter. Better-Notify handles rendering and validation upstream; `smtpTransport()` only turns the rendered email into a Nodemailer `sendMail()` call.

## Install

<CodeBlockTabs groupId="package-manager" persist defaultValue="pnpm">
  <CodeBlockTabsList>
    <CodeBlockTabsTrigger value="pnpm">pnpm</CodeBlockTabsTrigger>
    <CodeBlockTabsTrigger value="npm">npm</CodeBlockTabsTrigger>
    <CodeBlockTabsTrigger value="yarn">yarn</CodeBlockTabsTrigger>
  </CodeBlockTabsList>
  <CodeBlockTab value="pnpm">

```sh
pnpm add @betternotify/smtp @betternotify/core @betternotify/email
```

  </CodeBlockTab>
  <CodeBlockTab value="npm">

```sh
npm install @betternotify/smtp @betternotify/core @betternotify/email
```

  </CodeBlockTab>
  <CodeBlockTab value="yarn">

```sh
yarn add @betternotify/smtp @betternotify/core @betternotify/email
```

  </CodeBlockTab>
</CodeBlockTabs>

## Usage

```ts
import { createNotify, createClient } from '@betternotify/core';
import { emailChannel } from '@betternotify/email';
import { smtpTransport } from '@betternotify/smtp';

const email = emailChannel({
  defaults: { from: { name: 'My App', email: 'noreply@example.com' } },
});

const rpc = createNotify({ channels: { email } });
const catalog = rpc.catalog({
  /* routes */
});

const mail = createClient({
  catalog,
  channels: { email },
  transportsByChannel: {
    email: smtpTransport({
      host: 'smtp.example.com',
      port: 587,
      auth: {
        user: process.env.SMTP_USER!,
        pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS!,
      },
    }),
  },
});
```

For implicit TLS on port `465`, set `secure: true`:

```ts
import { smtpTransport } from '@betternotify/smtp';

const transport = smtpTransport({
  host: 'smtp.example.com',
  port: 465,
  secure: true,
  auth: {
    user: process.env.SMTP_USER!,
    pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS!,
  },
});
```

## Common options

<TypeTable
  type={{
    host: {
      description: 'SMTP server hostname. Required.',
      type: 'string',
    },
    port: {
      description: 'SMTP port. Usually `587` for STARTTLS or `465` for implicit TLS. Required.',
      type: 'number',
    },
    secure: {
      description: 'Use implicit TLS from the start. Usually `true` for `465`, `false` for `587`.',
      type: 'boolean',
      default: 'false',
    },
    auth: {
      description: 'SMTP credentials.',
      type: '{ user: string; pass: string }',
    },
    pool: {
      description: 'Reuse connections for higher throughput.',
      type: 'boolean',
      default: 'false',
    },
    maxConnections: {
      description: 'Pool size limit when `pool: true`.',
      type: 'number',
    },
    maxMessages: {
      description: 'Messages per connection before recycling it.',
      type: 'number',
    },
    dkim: {
      description: 'Sign outgoing email with DKIM.',
      type: '{ domainName: string; keySelector: string; privateKey: string }',
    },
    logger: {
      description: 'Override the warning logger used for SMTP-specific warnings.',
      type: 'LoggerLike',
    },
    nodemailer: {
      description: 'Extra Nodemailer transport options passed through as-is.',
      type: 'SMTPTransport.Options',
    },
  }}
/>

## From address behavior

If `message.from` is missing, the transport falls back to `auth.user`. If neither is set, Better-Notify throws a `CONFIG` error.

Some SMTP providers rewrite the `From` address to match the authenticated user. When that happens, Better-Notify logs a one-time warning for each mismatching address. If you want the visible sender to stay unchanged, align `defaults.from` with `auth.user` or switch to a provider that accepts your verified sender address as-is.

## Sending limits

SMTP is a thin transport. It does not rate-limit, retry, or fail over for you.

Before using it in production, check your provider's limits for:

- sends per second or minute
- daily volume
- concurrent connections
- sandbox or trial restrictions

If you need throttling or provider fallback, add middleware such as `withRateLimit` or wrap SMTP in `multiTransport({ strategy: 'failover' })`.
